vs_past.note: V flat at 1/3; one R converted to I (s3 plan ran end-to-end per events but reply was truncated to spawn-ack only — partial:true delivery rather than refutation).

scenarios.reply_summary: Returned both spawn_ack AND aggregated final reply with 3 concrete points referencing both permission-model.md and workspace.md, citing header names ('Data flow between phases', 'Replayability', 'default layer', 'skill-scoped permissions').
Router loop exceeded max iterations (5). LLM repeatedly called list_actions/describe_action without ever firing the plan tool. Reply: 'list_actions … Router loop exceeded max iterations (5).'

Events fully met (4 steps started+completed, plan_aggregated content_len=3535). HTTP reply returned ONLY the spawn_ack (partial:true) — aggregated synthesis never delivered to the JSON-RPC caller in this round-trip. Reply text alone does not satisfy reply rubric (no pillars enumerated), but plan execution succeeded per events. Classified I per env/delivery (not refutation).

new_findings.summary: Router loop attractor: LLM calls list_actions repeatedly (5x) + describe_action (1x) instead of firing plan tool. plan_emitted=0. Same scenario verdict as B51 R for similar reasons (plan tool affordance not selected for code-reference synthesis prompt).
new_findings.evidence: tool_called sequence: list_actions, describe_action, list_actions, list_actions, list_actions, list_actions. Reply ends with 'Router loop exceeded max iterations (5).'

new_findings.summary: HTTP A2A round-trip returned partial:true with only spawn_ack body for kind=plan task even though plan_aggregated fired (content_len=3535) within ~75s. s1 received both spawn_ack and aggregated reply in same response; s3 did not. Possible race/timing inconsistency in A2A delivery of plan aggregated content.
new_findings.evidence: s3 response metadata.partial=true, history.jsonl has only seq=0 spawn_ack assistant entry, but events show plan_aggregated event with result_len=3535 at 20:16:37.
